message:
style(vdbe): fix indentation of REPLACE secondary-index delete body after flattening nested if let

Cosmetic follow-up to the "feat(parser,core,vdbe):
schema-qualified TableStar end-to-end + collation-aware REPLACE
index delete path" commit in this batch.  That commit collapsed
the old
    if let Some(sc) = self.storage_cursors.get_mut(&meta.cursor_id) {
        if sc.writable && sc.cursor.index_move_to(...)?.is_found() {
            ...body...
        }
    }
into a single
    if found && let Some(sc) = ... && sc.writable { ...body... }
using let-chains, but the body (sc.cursor.delete + the cache
invalidation call) was left at its original indentation level
(one extra tab) from the nested form.  The resulting file
still compiled but the indentation was off by one level, which
rustfmt --check would flag.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs

- Reindent the body of the flattened `if ... { ... }` to match
  its single-level enclosing brace.  No functional change:
    sc.cursor.delete(&sc.cx)?;
    invalidate_storage_cursor_row_cache_with_reason(
        sc,
        self.collect_vdbe_metrics,
        DecodeCacheInvalidationReason::WriteMutation,
    );
- Pure whitespace; `cargo fmt --check` now passes clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
